response.justification: Candidate is a strong generalist senior developer with breadth across web, mobile, and embedded work, but the role explicitly requires 8 years of C++ programming and the resume shows only about 6 months of C++/C work at National Instruments (Dec 2014–Jun 2015). Most career depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not C++. Distributed-systems exposure exists (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) but is DLang-based, and there is no evidence of leading a cloud compute platform or formal tech-lead mentoring at the scale described.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development experience
Distributed, low-latency systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests/sec
Demonstrated mentoring and community leadership via workshops, dojos, and Selenium courses
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ requirement — only a short C/C++ stint at National Instruments
No evidence of production cloud compute platform architecture or DevOps/cloud engineer mentoring
No formal Technical Lead title; leadership signal is mostly community/teaching, not org-level platform ownership
